name: Joist Contractor Estimates & Invoices API
what_it_does: Joist (EverCommerce) contractor estimating, invoicing, and payment REST API for small residential contractors and home improvement professionals. Enables AI agents to manage estimate creation and line item management for contractor proposal automation, handle invoice generation and payment collection for contractor billing automation, access client and job management for contractor CRM automation, retrieve payment processing and digital payment acceptance for contractor cash flow automation, manage photo and document attachment for job documentation automation, handle customer e-signature for digital contract execution automation, access job scheduling and appointment management for contractor calendar automation, retrieve business reporting and revenue tracking for contractor financial intelligence, manage material and labor line item pricing for estimate accuracy automation, and integrate Joist with QuickBooks, Stripe, and other small business platforms.
use_cases: Creating estimates from contractor sales agents
Generating invoices from billing automation agents
Processing payments from contractor payment agents
Managing jobs from small contractor operations agents
not_for: Large commercial general contractors (use Procore or Autodesk)
Complex construction accounting with job costing
Union payroll and prevailing wage compliance
best_when: A small residential or light commercial contractor (1-25 employees) in home improvement, remodeling, painting, landscaping, or general trades wanting AI agents to automate estimate creation, invoice generation, payment collection, and client management.
avoid_when: CONTRACTOR LICENSE VERIFICATION BEFORE CLIENT ENGAGEMENT: Automated client onboarding and estimate delivery via Joist does not verify contractor licensing; automated marketing or estimate delivery for unlicensed contractor operations creates state contractor licensing violation and consumer protection exposure. MATERIAL PRICING ACCURACY IN AUTOMATED ESTIMATES: Automated estimate generation with Joist material price lists may use stale prices not reflecting current supplier costs; automated estimates with outdated material pricing create margin erosion when material costs exceed estimated amounts; implement real-time supplier price check for high-value estimates. STATE CONSUMER PROTECTION LAW FOR HOME IMPROVEMENT: Automated home improvement contract execution via Joist e-signature must comply with state-specific Home Improvement Contractor Acts (HICA) including right of rescission disclosure, contract element requirements, and permit disclosure; automated contract delivery without state-required disclosures creates consumer protection violation. DEPOSIT COLLECTION LIMITS: Automated deposit collection through Joist payment processing must comply with state contractor licensing law deposit limits; states including California, Florida, and New York cap initial deposits; automated collection above cap creates contractor license violation.

auth.notes: Joist uses OAuth 2.0 with scopes. REST API with JSON. Toronto, Ontario HQ (acquired by EverCommerce). Founded 2011 in Toronto. Subsidiary of EverCommerce (NASDAQ: EVCM). EverCommerce is a service commerce platform company. 1.3M+ contractors use Joist in US and Canada. Simple estimate, invoice, and payment for small home improvement contractors. Competes with Jobber, Housecall Pro, and Contractor+ for small contractor management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free basic plan with limited estimates/invoices; Pro plan required for payment processing and API access
pricing.paid_tiers: Pro plan ~$15-25/month per user; API access in Pro and above
pricing.notes: Toronto ON / EverCommerce NASDAQ:EVCM. Founded 2011. 1.3M+ small contractors. Free basic + Pro subscription. Small home improvement contractor focus.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client with scopes; estimate and invoice API throttled per subscription tier

known_agent_gotchas: HOME IMPROVEMENT CONTRACT STATE LAW DISCLOSURES: Automated home improvement contract generation via Joist must include state-specific HICA (Home Improvement Contractor Act) required elements including right of rescission notice, contractor license number, permit disclosure, and payment schedule; automated contract delivery without state-required disclosures creates consumer protection violation

MATERIAL PRICE LIST FRESHNESS FOR ESTIMATE ACCURACY: Joist material price catalog may not reflect current supplier pricing; automated estimates using Joist price list for volatile materials (lumber, copper, HVAC equipment) create margin risk when actual material costs exceed estimate; integrate real-time supplier pricing for high-value material line items

DEPOSIT COLLECTION STATE LAW COMPLIANCE: Automated customer deposit collection through Joist payment processing must check state contractor deposit limits; California, Florida, New York, and other states cap home improvement contractor deposits; automated collection above state limit creates contractor license violation and consumer fraud claim

EverCommerce acquisition API continuity — Joist was acquired by EverCommerce (NASDAQ: EVCM) and is one of multiple EverCommerce platforms; verify current API investment and product integration strategy within EverCommerce portfolio; platform consolidation may affect Joist-specific API stability

QuickBooks sync reconciliation — Joist QuickBooks Online integration syncs estimates and invoices; automated Joist-QuickBooks sync can create duplicate entries if the same transaction is manually entered in QuickBooks; implement sync conflict detection before automated financial data reliance

E-signature enforceability for large contracts — Joist e-signature meets ESIGN Act requirements for small residential contracts; for high-value home improvement contracts, verify state-specific enforceability and consider DocuSign or Adobe Sign for stronger audit trail on disputes
agent_readiness.security_notes: Small contractor management. SOC2. OAuth2. US/CA. Contractor estimate, invoice, and payment data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Contractor management. SOC2. OAuth2. US/CA. Estimate, invoice, and payment data.
